homework.title: Identifying Chemical Properties
homework.instructions: Select the correct option that best describes the property mentioned.
homework.question: Which of the following properties is a physical property of sodium?
options: It has a silvery-white luster and is soft
It burns in air to form an oxide
It displaces hydrogen from dilute acids

homework.title: Distinguishing Physical and Chemical Changes
homework.instructions: Read the scenarios below and classify them as physical or chemical changes. Provide one reason for each classification.
homework.question: Classify the following: (a) Melting of ice, (b) Rusting of iron, (c) Burning of a candle wax. Justify your answer.
homework.answerSpace: Write your classification and justification below.
homework.expectedResponse: (a) Physical - change in state only; (b) Chemical - new substance (iron oxide) formed; (c) Chemical - combustion produces CO2 and water.

homework.title: Reactivity Series Visualization
homework.instructions: Draw a vertical bar chart representing the reactivity series of metals (Potassium, Sodium, Calcium, Magnesium, Aluminum, Zinc, Iron, Copper). Label the most reactive and least reactive metals.
homework.question: Create a diagram showing the order of reactivity for Potassium, Sodium, Calcium, Magnesium, Aluminum, Zinc, Iron, and Copper. Indicate which metal would displace copper from its salt solution.
homework.answerSpace: Draw the chart and label the metals.
homework.visualRequirement: A vertical bar chart or list showing reactivity order from top (most reactive) to bottom (least reactive).
homework.expectedResponse: Diagram showing K > Na > Ca > Mg > Al > Zn > Fe > Cu. Metals above Copper (K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al, Zn, Fe) can displace Copper.

homework.title: Balancing Chemical Equations
homework.instructions: Balance the following chemical equations. Ensure the number of atoms of each element is equal on both sides.
homework.question: Balance the following equations: (a) Mg + O2 -> MgO, (b) Fe + H2O -> Fe3O4 + H2

homework.title: Real-World Corrosion Prevention
homework.instructions: Read the scenario and answer the questions based on chemical principles.
homework.question: A bridge made of iron is showing signs of rusting near the sea. Explain why this happens chemically and suggest two methods to prevent it, explaining the science behind each method.
homework.expectedResponse: Explanation: Iron reacts with moisture and oxygen in the air (especially salt water) to form hydrated iron oxide (rust). Prevention: 1. Painting/Greasing - creates a barrier against moisture/oxygen. 2. Galvanization - coating with zinc which acts as a sacrificial anode.

learningOutcomesCovered: Identify physical and chemical properties of substances
Distinguish between physical and chemical changes
Understand reactivity series and displacement reactions
